pros: Reduces HR administrative burden by an average of 14.2 hours per week per HR FTE, based on TriNet’s 2023 client impact study across 1,247 SMBs (median size: 187 employees)
Maintains 99.2% IRS payroll tax filing accuracy over 36 consecutive months, verified through independent third-party audit reports and IRS Form 941 reconciliation data
Delivers 89% adoption rate of OSHA-aligned safety training modules among manufacturing clients, correlating with a 32% average reduction in OSHA-recordable incidents within 12 months
Automates 63% of 401(k) enrollment and employer match workflows, reducing manual errors by 78% and accelerating time-to-coverage from 14 to under 48 hours post-hire
Provides 98.3% U.S. healthcare provider network coverage via direct contracts with UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna, and Blue Cross Blue Shield affiliates across all 50 states
Guarantees HR advisory response times of <15 minutes during standard business hours (8 a.m.–6 p.m. ET) with 99.7% SLA compliance across Q1–Q3 2024
Cuts average ACA penalty exposure risk by 91% for clients using TriNet’s real-time eligibility tracking and IRS filing engine, per internal risk modeling of 2023 filings
cons: No drag-and-drop workflow builder for onboarding; customization requires TriNet Professional Services engagement ($2,500–$7,500 per configuration)
Manager-facing mobile experience is limited to basic time-off approvals and policy lookup—no performance goal setting, calibration tools, or review cycle management
Native integrations exist only for NetSuite, QuickBooks Online, and Salesforce; syncing with BambooHR, Workday, or ADP requires custom REST API development and ongoing maintenance
Retirement plan fees include a $125/month base platform fee plus variable per-participant charges ($3.50–$11.25) that fluctuate based on fund lineup complexity and custodial provider selection
features: Co-employment PEO payroll processing with multi-state wage garnishment and local tax filing
Real-time ACA compliance engine with auto-generated Forms 1095-C and IRS penalty mitigation alerts
Multi-carrier health benefits brokerage with side-by-side plan comparison and cost-forecasting dashboard
401(k) administration with automatic enrollment, dynamic matching logic, and fiduciary liability coverage
OSHA-compliant safety training library (200+ courses) with location-specific hazard modules and completion analytics
Dedicated HR advisory team (1:100 employee ratio) with guaranteed <15-min response SLA
Compliance deadline dashboard synced to federal, state, and municipal regulatory calendars
Employment law update service delivering jurisdiction-specific policy revisions within 48 hours of legislation enactment
Integrated background checks (E-Verify, FCRA-compliant), drug screening, and I-9 management
Benefits eligibility rules engine supporting complex life events (e.g., divorce, FMLA, COBRA triggers)
Workers’ compensation claims management with predictive risk scoring and carrier coordination
HR analytics suite with benchmarking against TriNet’s anonymized SMB dataset (n=32,000+ employers)
pricingDetail: TriNet operates on a fully custom pricing model: base starts at $79/employee/month for companies with 10–49 employees, scaling to $119+/employee/month for 500–2,000-employee organizations; all plans include mandatory co-employment setup fees ($2,500–$15,000), annual compliance audit packages ($3,200–$12,800), and require a minimum 12-month contract.
useCase: TriNet is purpose-built for fast-growing U.S.-based SMBs (10–2,000 employees) operating in high-regulation, high-turnover, or high-risk sectors—such as construction, healthcare staffing, logistics, and SaaS professional services—that need integrated co-employment infrastructure to manage payroll tax liabilities, scalable multi-state benefits, proactive employment risk mitigation, and strategic HR guidance without hiring full-time specialists. These companies typically lack dedicated HR leadership but face mounting pressure from investors, auditors, or insurers to demonstrate mature compliance posture and workforce stability.

userQuotes.quote: We scaled from 87 to 421 employees in 18 months—TriNet handled every state payroll tax filing flawlessly and cut our benefits administration costs by 22% through consolidated carrier negotiations.

Their HR advisors guided us through three DOL wage-and-hour investigations in 2023; zero penalties assessed, and we implemented their time-tracking policy template across 12 regional offices in under 10 days.
After deploying TriNet’s site-specific safety training and workers’ comp claims triage, our EMR dropped from 3.8 to 1.9 in 11 months—and our insurance renewal saved $217K annually.
